title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · System Lens: Repository Trust
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux repository trust increment in a distribution-aware package change record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Repository Trust
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 2.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 2.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Repository Trust contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.

content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 2.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 2.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Repository Trust condition decided your prediction.

content.value: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide — Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

options.text: Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
HTTPS alone proves a package repository is trustworthy. This does not resolve predict repository trust.

For predict repository trust, apply Repository Trust without its stated preconditions, omit the a distribution-aware package change record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Repository Trust for a distribution-aware package change record.

content.text: Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Repository Trust while producing a distribution-aware package change record.
